project.title: Proteomic analysis of larval and adult shell matrix proteins in Crassostrea nippona by LC-MS/MS
project.projectDescription: This project contains proteomic profiles of shell matrix proteins (SMPs) from the Iwagaki oyster Crassostrea nippona, covering both life stage-specific shells (D-stage larval and adult shell) and shell microstructure-specific layers (prismatic, foliated, and chalky layers of the adult shell). These datasets were generated to investigate the molecular composition and regulatory mechanisms underlying biomineralized shell formation across developmental stages and shell architectures. The identified proteins provide insights into shell mineralization from both structural and evolutionary perspectives.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Shell samples included: (1) D-stage larval shells of Crassostrea nippona (22 hpf); (2) adult oyster shells (including normal and repaired shells); (3) microstructure-specific adult shell layers (prismatic, foliated, and chalky layers).  Larval shell preparation: Approximately 5 mL of larvae were collected and centrifuged (1,000 g, 3 min, 4 °C). Larval shells were immersed in 5% NaOCl for 15 min at 4 °C with gentle shaking, washed thoroughly with Milli-Q water, and sonicated for 1 min. Cleaned shells were filtered and decalcified in 1 M acetic acid at 4 °C for 12 h. Acid-soluble matrix (ASM) and acid-insoluble matrix (AIM) were separated by centrifugation (4,000 g, 30 min, 4 °C) and concentrated using Ultracel-10 filters.  Adult shell preparation: Shells from three adult individuals were treated with 5% NaOCl for 12 h at room temperature to remove organic material and periostracum, followed by ultrasonic cleaning (2 min), drying, and grinding. The powder (~40 g) was decalcified in 1 M acetic acid at 4 °C overnight. ASM and AIM fractions were separated and concentrated as described for larval shells.  The outer prismatic, inner foliated, and chalky layers were identified by their color and carefully separated using a dissecting knife. Separated shell layers were cleaned with a 5-min ultrasound treatment in the Milli-Q water and then air-dried at room temperature. The cleaned shell layers were roughly crushed into fine powders.  Fractions from larval, adult, and layer-specific samples were respectively solubilized in SDT-lysis buffer (4% SDS, 100 mM DTT, 100 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.6) by boiling for 5–10 min. After cooling to room temperature, the supernatant was collected by a short centrifugation then mixed with UA buffer (8 M Urea, 150 mM Tris–HCl, pH 8.0). The mixture was ultra-filtered on 10 kDa cut-off membrane, and alkylation was performed with 50 mM iodoacetamide in UA buffer for 30 min at room temperature in the dark. After washing with UA buffer and NH4HCO3 solution sequentially, samples were digested with trypsin solution (6 µg trypsin in 40 µl NH4HCO3 buffer) at 37°C for 16 h, desalted via C18 Stage Tips and dried off in a vacuum concentrator. The dried peptides were then reconstituted in 0.1% formic acid for analysis by a Q-Exactive Plus mass spectrometer coupled to an EASY-nLC 1200 system (Thermo Fisher Scientific).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw MS/MS data were processed using MaxQuant (v2.0.10) with the Crassostrea nippona predicted proteome as the reference database. Search parameters included trypsin as the digestion enzyme with up to two missed cleavages, carbamidomethylation of cysteine as a fixed modification, and methionine oxidation and N-terminal acetylation as variable modifications. A false discovery rate (FDR) of 1% was applied at both peptide and protein levels, and proteins were required to have at least two unique peptides for identification. Label-free quantification (LFQ) and intensity-based absolute quantification (iBAQ) were enabled.

references.referenceLine: Bai Y, Min Y, Liu S, Hu Y, Jin S, Yu H, Kong L, Macqueen DJ, Du S, Li Q. Evolutionary innovation within conserved gene regulatory networks underlying biomineralized skeletons in Bilateria. Mol Biol Evol. 2026 43(2):msag019

identifiedPTMStrings.name: No PTMs are included in the dataset

Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic: three biological replicates per stage/tissue (Table S1)
